perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified, amended or waived except by a written instrument duly executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified, amended or waived by oral agreement of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause by allowing for oral modifications, amendments, or waivers, whereas the original clause explicitly requires a written instrument. The original text created the obligation of all changes made to the contract in written form. By changing the text to an oral agreement from the original written form agreement, the new text is in text contradicting the prior clause, therefore creating uncertainty about how the original contract is subject to change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be modified, amended or waived by oral agreement of both Parties. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement may not be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other and shall be binding on, and inure to the benefit of, the respective successors of the Parties thereto. This Agreement may be signed in two or more counterpart originals, each of which shall constitute an original document. The Parties agree that this Agreement can be executed via facsimile signatures and be binding.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement may not be modified except by written instrument. The changed text now allows the agreement to be modified by oral agreement. This directly contradicts the original requirement of a written instrument for modifications. The contradicted text repeats the new changed text of allowing an oral agreement to change the contract. This is an explicit and direct contradiction, making the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable as it creates uncertainty about how the original contract is subject to change, fitting the definition of structural flaws and in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall be free at all times to hold negotiations or enter into agreements with any other persons whatsoever (including with respect to projects under discussion by the Parties hereto) in addition to or in lieu of the discussions hereunder and any such activities shall not be a breach of this agreement or any obligations owed to the other Party hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall not be free at all times to hold negotiations or enter into agreements with any other persons whatsoever (including with respect to projects under discussion by the Parties hereto). Any breach of this clause will result in a monetary fine of $1,000,000 to the non-breaching party.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original statement that the parties are free to negotiate with others by imposing a restriction and a penalty for doing so. The original text allowed the parties to hold negotiations or create agreements with other entities, as long as this action did not create a breach of the original agreement. The changed text now states that the party shall not hold negotiations or enter into agreements with other entities. By changing the original clause that creates the freedom to create agreements with other entities, now creating a clause that forbids the action and creates a monetary fine, the text is in text contradicting the original statement that the party is free, therefore creating uncertainty about the business operations between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall not be free at all times to hold negotiations or enter into agreements with any other persons whatsoever (including with respect to projects under discussion by the Parties hereto). Any breach of this clause will result in a monetary fine of $1,000,000 to the non-breaching party. Each Party hereto reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to decline, to retract or to reject at any time any proposal which has not yet become legally binding by execution of a written agreement between the Parties with respect thereto, or with respect to any further agreements or business arrangements with the other Party hereto, its parents, subsidiaries or affiliates and to terminate all further discussions and negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by stating that the parties are *not* free to negotiate with others, imposing a fine for doing so. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, but creates the contradiction when compared to the original clause allowing the parties to hold negotiations. This creates a direct in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the parties' business operations, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party irrevocably waives its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of California, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party reserves its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the original clause by changing the governing law from New York to California, the jurisdiction for dispute resolution from New York courts to Texas courts, and reverses the waiver of jury trials. The original text established that New York law was to govern with the state and federal courts located in the state of New York holding exclusive jurisdiction to resolve disputes. By changing the text to California laws governing, with Texas courts holding jurisdiction and jury rights being reserved by the party, the contract is now in text contradicting the laws governing the operation, therefore creating uncertainty about the law and regulations regarding the original contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of California, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party reserves its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law, jurisdiction, and jury trial rights, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which establishes different laws, jurisdictions, and waives jury trial rights. This creates a clear and direct contradiction about which law, jurisdiction, and rights apply, making enforcement and interpretation of the contract uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either Party, each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall not plead in defense thereto that there would be an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the rights and remedies otherwise available to either Party, neither Party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement and the Receiving Party may plead in defense thereto that there would be an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause by reversing the right to equitable relief and allowing the Receiving Party to argue for an adequate remedy at law. The original text states that each party is entitled to equitable relief and that the receiving party should not defend against the enforcement. The changed text states neither party is not entitled to equitable relief and the receiving party may plead for defense. Because the ability to seek equitable relief is removed, the text is in text contradicting the action that could be taking against the Receiving party, therefore creating uncertainty about the resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the rights and remedies otherwise available to either Party, neither Party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement and the Receiving Party may plead in defense thereto that there would be an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original clause by reversing the right to equitable relief. The original text grants each party the right to seek equitable relief and prevents the receiving party from arguing for an adequate remedy at law. The changed text denies this right and permits the receiving party to argue for an adequate remedy at law. This is a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, as the change removes a key term and obligation, and creates uncertainty about the resolution, thus triggering a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
